frame_analyses.description: Title card 'Roeder's Knot' displayed with BEST Institute watermark. No suture or instruments visible. Didactic slide.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional framing; no operative content yet.

frame_analyses.description: Same title card; red suture beginning to appear in lower frame center.
Red suture extending vertically from bottom of frame; still in introduction phase.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture being positioned; no instrument grasp yet visible.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture fully extended vertically; appears to be straight vertical line (target Penrose drain representation).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture fully present; appears to represent drain baseline.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture vertical; no change in configuration.
Red suture beginning to curve at top; first loop formation initiating. One end bending upward and to the right.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw beginning; single loop forming above vertical stem.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture forming partial loop; top portion curving to right. Loop approximately 40% formed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw mid-formation; clean curve geometry.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture with loop approximately 60% formed; right arm curved upward and rightward.
Red suture with single loop complete; clean U-shaped loop above vertical stem.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First single throw completed and formed.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture with first loop still visible; second throw beginning. Right arm of first loop starting to cross over vertical stem.
Red suture showing both loops beginning to intertwine. First loop arm crossing down and left; second loop forming.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot geometry becoming visible; two throws overlapping.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture knot with three distinct loops visible; complex interweaving pattern. Knot density increasing.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Multiple throws now stacked; knot architecture taking shape.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture knot with four visible loop portions; tight interweaving at center. Knot becoming progressively more compact.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot structure maturing; three throws likely complete or near completion.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture knot fully formed with multiple interlocking loops; compact, well-defined geometry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears complete; clean knot structure with no slippage visible.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture knot static; identical to frame 14. Knot at rest position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot holding tension; no movement or slippage.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture knot in identical position; vertical stem continues below knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot secured; ready for assessment or cutting phase.

frame_analyses.description: Red suture knot and stem unchanged; holding position.
Red suture knot identical configuration; video appears to loop or hold final state.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot state: compact, well-formed, no slippage visible.

knot_assessments.description: First single throw formed cleanly from frames 6–9; U-shaped loop with good geometry.
Second throw begins at frame 10 and integrates at frame 11; overlaps first throw appropriately.
Third throw appears to complete by frame 12–13; knot density increases markedly.
knot_assessments.notes: Final knot compact and well-formed; no slippage observed.

suture_placement.note: No actual suture passage through marked Penrose drain targets is visible in this instructional animation. This is a stylized diagram of Roeder's knot tied extracorporeally.

drain_assessment.note: No actual drain visible; animation shows red suture line representing the concept of a drain. Slit closure cannot be assessed from this diagram.

score_components.note: This is an instructional/educational animation demonstrating Roeder's knot technique, not a performance video of an actual FLS task4 completion. Scoring cannot be applied per standard FLS rubric because: (1) No actual suture is passed through marked targets on a real Penrose drain; (2) No instruments (needle drivers, knot pusher, scissors) are shown; (3) No actual completion time is measurable in a didactic context; (4) Slit closure and drain integrity cannot be assessed; (5) Video does not meet 'performance' or 'expert_demo' classification criteria.

confidence_rationale: This is classified as instructional content and therefore cannot be scored using the FLS task4 rubric. The video is a stylized animation of a knot-tying technique (Roeder's knot) with no operative context, no actual equipment, no real drain, and no measurable task completion. Classification overrides performance scoring.
cannot_determine: Completion time — animation has no clear start or end markers for FLS timing
Suture passage accuracy — no actual Penrose drain or marked targets present
Drain slit closure — no drain present; only schematic suture line shown
Knot pusher usage — no instruments visible; animation only
Actual knot security under tension — animation static; no tension testing shown
video_content_summary: This is an instructional animation demonstrating the formation of Roeder's knot with a single red suture strand representing a drain. The animation progresses smoothly from a vertical stem through three single throws (frames 6–13), resulting in a compact, well-formed final knot. While the knot geometry appears correct for teaching purposes, this is not an FLS task4 performance video and therefore does not receive an FLS score.

demonstrated_techniques: Clean loop geometry with proper spacing
Interweaving of multiple throws to form compact knot
teaching_content.instructional_value: This animation clearly illustrates the step-by-step construction of Roeder's knot, a secure extracorporeal knot used in laparoscopic surgery. It is suitable for trainees learning knot-tying mechanics before performing on actual task boxes.
